message:
fix(share,tools): iterative dir traversal, colon-delimited LLM providers, and saturating timestamp math

Convert collect_entries() in bundle.rs from recursive to iterative
stack-based directory traversal to match the same stack-overflow
prevention applied to retention.rs. Behavior is identical.

Accept colon-delimited provider prefixes (e.g. "groq:llama-3.3-70b")
in addition to slash-delimited ones for Groq, DeepSeek, xAI, and
OpenRouter LLM endpoints. Some agent frameworks pass model identifiers
with colon separators; both forms now resolve correctly.

Add absolute path detection for Windows-style paths in
detect_suspicious_file_reservation using std::path::Path::is_absolute()
in addition to the existing Unix leading-slash check.

Replace bare timestamp subtraction with saturating_sub in
force_release_file_reservation stale-signal calculations to prevent
underflow when agent last_active_ts is ahead of server clock.

Update doctor_check.json test fixture to reflect bash shell detection
instead of zsh (matches current CI environment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
